name: matchms
tagline: Reference skill wrapping the matchms Python library for mass-spec spectral similarity scoring and compound ID.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped documentation skill. No credential access, no network egress beyond a normal `pip install matchms` of the real PyPI package, no shell-piped remote code, no obfuscation, and no attempts to steer the reviewing agent. Behavior matches its stated scope. Only minor gaps: it doesn't spell out failure-path handling (e.g. malformed spectra files)

highlights.summary: No explicit statement of expected output shape (e.g. what a Scores object or plot should look like beyond print statements); acceptable for a library-reference skill but not spelled out.
No guidance on handling malformed spectra, missing peaks, empty libraries, or similarity-function errors — a user following the skill has no fallback described for common failure modes.
